description: Greedy line wrapping modelled on Python's stdlib textwrap module: wrap/shorten over word-length lists, with lineWidth a frozen helper (the sum of a line's word-lengths, exposed so specs can reference it directly — not a fillable API). Specs span witness/bound/maximality plus closure, boundary, count-bound and width-monotonicity laws for wrap; base/fit-closure/length/maximality laws for shorten; and a cross-API law identifying wrap's first line with shorten — pinning the greedy first-fit answer, anchored on List.sum/List.flatten/List.IsPrefix. A global-optimality block (over a frozen IsValidWrapping predicate) adds the deep theorems: wrap is itself a valid wrapping, wrap is line-count optimal among all valid wrappings (greedy stays-ahead/exchange), total word-width is conserved, the line count is bounded below by totalWidth/width, and re-wrapping the flattened output is a fixpoint. A further deep exchange/structural block adds the extensive-effort obligations whose proofs need long induction/realignment arguments: greedy stays-ahead pointwise on consumed words against every valid wrapping; lexicographic-maximality / uniqueness — wrap is THE valid wrapping that is also greedy-maximal (frozen IsGreedyMaximal); optimal substructure (wrap chunks = firstLine :: wrap leftover); line-count monotonicity under appending words; combined feasibility+optimality; and a two-sided ceil(totalLen/w) ≤ #lines ≤ #chunks count bound. A final discovered-invariant block adds clean end-facts whose proofs require GENERALIZING the goal before induction closes — the invariant is deliberately not revealed by the statement: first-line word-count maximality among all valid wrappings (proved via greedy = longest fitting prefix); cumulative-width stays-ahead (greedy maximizes total packed width in its first k lines, reduced to a prefix-sum domination controlled by the word-count stays-ahead); drop-last optimal substructure from the back (deleting greedy's last line = greedy of the front words, via a suffix-stability engine); and a tight pairwise-width line-count bound ((#lines − 1)·w ≤ 2·totalWidth, summing the adjacency invariant over disjoint consecutive line pairs). A further generalized-substructure / composition block adds more strengthened-IH obligations: generalized optimal substructure (dropping the first k output lines = greedy of the words after them, the arbitrary-k lift of the k=1 law); line-count subadditivity under concatenation (#wrap(xs++ys) ≤ #wrap xs + #wrap ys, via a CONSTRUCTED competitor wrapping wrap xs ++ wrap ys fed to the exchange theorem); append suffix-stability (every output line of wrap xs except its last reappears verbatim at the front of wrap(xs++ys)); exact append line-count realignment (#wrap(xs++ys) = (#wrap xs − 1) + #wrap(lastLine ++ ys), splicing the trailing open line with the appended words); and a shorten deeper-law cluster — shorten width-maximality over all fitting prefixes, shorten word-count monotonicity in the width, shorten input-prefix monotonicity, shorten locality (only one lookahead word past the kept run matters), and shorten idempotence. Scope: the model is greedy first-fit packing over word-LENGTHS (`List Nat`), not full `textwrap.wrap` with its keyword arguments — `lineWidth` is a frozen helper (the sum of a line's word lengths), and over-long-word splitting, whitespace/separator handling, and placeholder/max_lines behavior are out of scope.
